title: SACRAMENTO – Legislative Republicans are calling on the California Air Resources Board to be honest and transparent about the impact a series of proposed regulations will have on C
prompt_preview:
[1 chunks] Task: Extract all distinct claims about AI or emerging technology in this press release. Do NOT assume a fixed number.
Output JSON only matching the provided schema.
Return concise, standalone claim_texts that could be tested against scientific literature. Exclude purely procedural statem

claims.claim_text: The California Air Resources Board projected that an update to the Low Carbon Fuel Standard could raise gas prices by more than $0.50 cents a gallon.
Legislative Republicans allege that the California Air Resources Board is not being honest about the impact of proposed regulations on consumers.
The proposed changes to the cap-and-trade program could impose expensive new restrictions.
High gas prices in California are described as a basic necessity issue rather than a luxury.
If families pay more to fill up their gas tank, they will have less money available for food, bills, or savings.
The Newsom administration is facing criticism for potentially increasing costs for consumers.
